title: Side-Step Kiss
setup: Your queen on **f7** and king on **f6** form a tight team — but landing on {{f8}} would be a stalemate trap.
hint: Don't take the obvious file — slide one square sideways onto a square your king already defends.
explanation:
## The Idea

A **kiss-of-death corner mate**. Going to {{f8}} just gives check with an escape; the right move is to drop *next to* the king on a square your king defends.

Play {{Qg7#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- The queen on g7 attacks g8 directly (check)
- {{h8}} — covered by the queen on g7 (diagonal)
- {{h7}} — covered by the queen on g7 (rank)
- {{Kxg7}} is illegal — your king on **f6** defends g7
- Nothing can block: the queen is adjacent

> **Takeaway:** When you have a queen + king mating, count flight squares for *every* candidate move. The right square is often the one *defended by your king*, not the one giving check from afar.
